sysmon_event_ids.name: Process creation
sysmon_event_ids.description: Provides extended information about a newly created process, including the full command line, user context, process GUID, hashes, and parent process details.

sysmon_event_ids.name: A process changed a file creation time
sysmon_event_ids.description: Registered when a process explicitly modifies a file's creation time. This is commonly used by attackers for timestomping to disguise malicious files.

sysmon_event_ids.name: Network connection
sysmon_event_ids.description: Logs TCP/UDP connections on the machine, detailing the source and destination IP addresses, ports, hostnames, and the process initiating the connection.

sysmon_event_ids.name: Sysmon service state changed
sysmon_event_ids.description: Reports the status of the Sysmon service itself, capturing events when the service starts, stops, or experiences an error.

sysmon_event_ids.name: Process terminated
sysmon_event_ids.description: Logs when a process terminates, providing the exact time, process ID, and process image name to assist in tracking process lifecycles.

sysmon_event_ids.name: Driver loaded
sysmon_event_ids.description: Provides information about a driver being loaded into the system, including signature verification status and cryptographic hashes.

sysmon_event_ids.name: Image loaded
sysmon_event_ids.description: Logs when a module or Dynamic Link Library (DLL) is loaded into a specific process. Highly useful for detecting DLL hijacking and injection.

sysmon_event_ids.name: CreateRemoteThread
sysmon_event_ids.description: Detects when a process creates a thread in another process. This technique is extensively used by malware for process injection.

sysmon_event_ids.name: RawAccessRead
sysmon_event_ids.description: Detects when a process conducts raw read operations from the drive using the \\.\ file system format, a technique often used to bypass built-in security locks.

sysmon_event_ids.name: ProcessAccess
sysmon_event_ids.description: Logs when a process opens a handle to another process, often an indicator of memory dumping tools (like LSASS dumping) or memory injection.

sysmon_event_ids.name: File created
sysmon_event_ids.description: Identifies when a file is created or overwritten. This is critical for tracking dropped malware binaries or staging folders.

sysmon_event_ids.name: RegistryEvent (Object creation and deletion)
sysmon_event_ids.description: Tracks the creation and deletion of Registry keys and values by system processes.

sysmon_event_ids.name: RegistryEvent (Value Set)
sysmon_event_ids.description: Tracks modifications to Registry values. Crucial for detecting persistence mechanisms like modifications to Run/RunOnce keys.

sysmon_event_ids.name: RegistryEvent (Key and Value Rename)
sysmon_event_ids.description: Logs when a Registry key or value is renamed, which can be an obfuscation tactic to bypass specific security monitoring rules.

sysmon_event_ids.name: FileCreateStreamHash
sysmon_event_ids.description: Logs when an Alternate Data Stream (ADS) is created on a file. It generates a hash of the stream contents, capturing files downloaded with a 'Zone.Identifier' mark.

sysmon_event_ids.name: ServiceConfigurationChange
sysmon_event_ids.description: Logs changes made to the configuration of Sysmon itself, such as when the filtering rules inside the configuration file are updated.

sysmon_event_ids.name: PipeEvent (Pipe Created)
sysmon_event_ids.description: Logs the creation of a named pipe, which is frequently utilized for Inter-Process Communication (IPC) by attackers and administrative tools.

sysmon_event_ids.name: PipeEvent (Pipe Connected)
sysmon_event_ids.description: Logs when a connection is established to an existing named pipe, mapping communications between processes.

sysmon_event_ids.name: WmiEvent (WmiEventFilter activity detected)
sysmon_event_ids.description: Logs the registration of a WMI event filter, a primary method used by advanced adversaries to establish persistent execution on a host.

sysmon_event_ids.name: WmiEvent (WmiEventConsumer activity detected)
sysmon_event_ids.description: Logs the registration of WMI event consumers, mapping out the physical actions or payloads tied to a WMI event trigger.

sysmon_event_ids.name: WmiEvent (WmiEventConsumerToFilter activity detected)
sysmon_event_ids.description: Logs when a consumer is securely bound to a filter, completing the end-to-end setup of a active WMI persistence mechanism.

sysmon_event_ids.name: DNS Query
sysmon_event_ids.description: Logs when a process executes a DNS query, capturing the queried domain name, the result code, and the process responsible for the query.

sysmon_event_ids.name: File Delete
sysmon_event_ids.description: Logs when a file is deleted from the system, assisting analysts in tracking malware that attempts to securely erase its own footprints.

sysmon_event_ids.name: File Executable Detected
sysmon_event_ids.description: Logs when a newly created file is explicitly recognized as an executable binary based on its internal structural format.

sysmon_event_ids.name: Error
sysmon_event_ids.description: Generated when an internal error occurs within the Sysmon service, often indicating missing resources or extreme system loads.
